channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: House Democrats accuse Trump of 'hijacking' America's 250th birthday for his own gain
item.description: A 55-page report from House Democrats accuses Freedom 250 of America's birthday celebrations for profit, using questionable fundraising methods. Watchdog groups had already sounded alarms.
item.content:encoded:
A 55-page report from House Democrats accuses Freedom 250 of America's birthday celebrations for profit, using questionable fundraising methods. Watchdog groups had already sounded alarms.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Thoughts on the Nation's Birthday in Bristol, Rhode Island
item.description: We talk with Heidi Vermilyea, who runs the souvenir stand for the Bristol, R.I., Fourth of July celebration, the nation's oldest observance of the holiday.
item.content:encoded: We talk with Heidi Vermilyea, who runs the souvenir stand for the Bristol, R.I., Fourth of July celebration, the nation's oldest observance of the holiday.
item.dc:creator: David Wright

item.title: Christian missionaries have found a new (virtual) mission territory
item.description: The room may be virtual, but the prayer — and the evangelism — are not.
item.content:encoded:
The room may be virtual, but the prayer — and the evangelism — are not.

(Image credit: Via Geoffery Powell)
item.dc:creator: Fiona Murphy

item.title: Even fans who don't speak Spanish are watching the World Cup on Telemundo. Here's why
item.description: They may not understand every word, but fans appreciate exuberant announcers who match their enthusiasm. Plus, cameras stay on the field during hydration breaks rather than cutting to commercials.
item.content:encoded:
They may not understand every word, but fans appreciate exuberant announcers who match their enthusiasm. Plus, cameras stay on the field during hydration breaks rather than cutting to commercials.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: 'Opting Out' is a graphic novel of 7th grade survival from the author of 'Gender Queer'
item.description: Saachi, 12, is a head-strong, confident 6th grader when the book opens, but soon she finds herself increasingly frustrated as the social dynamics of elementary school seem to unravel before her eyes.
item.content:encoded: Saachi, 12, is a head-strong, confident 6th grader when the book opens, but soon she finds herself increasingly frustrated as the social dynamics of elementary school seem to unravel before her eyes.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman

item.title: In Kennedy Ryan's romance, happily-ever-after is for everyone
item.description: The romance books Ryan read growing up rarely included characters who looked like her. Now she deliberately centers people the genre has left out, including Black, Indigenous and queer women.
item.content:encoded:
Score, follows two former college sweethearts reunited while making a film about the Harlem Renaissance.'/>

The romance books Ryan read growing up rarely included characters who looked like her. Now she deliberately centers people the genre has left out, including Black, Indigenous and queer women.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Here's why comic Craig Ferguson became an 'American On Purpose'
item.description: The Scottish-born comic became a U.S. citizen in 2008. He showcases his goofy sense of humor, and his appetite for the unpredictable, in a new five-part documentary series for CNN.
item.content:encoded: The Scottish-born comic became a U.S. citizen in 2008. He showcases his goofy sense of humor, and his appetite for the unpredictable, in a new five-part documentary series for CNN.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Greetings from London, where Banksy's flag man is a warning cry
item.description: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.content:encoded: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Ni

item.title: Longtime public servant 'guardedly patriotic' as America turns 250
item.description: At age 72, Houston-area county commissioner Rodney Ellis has seen a lot of both progress and setbacks for America. He's celebrating America's birthday but has concerns about her future.
item.content:encoded: At age 72, Houston-area county commissioner Rodney Ellis has seen a lot of both progress and setbacks for America. He's celebrating America's birthday but has concerns about her future.
item.dc:creator: John Burnett

item.title: How does the Kennedy Center board make decisions? This legal filing sheds some light
item.description: Last year, Trump dismissed Biden-appointed Kennedy Center board members and replaced them with loyalists. A new filing by the Center's lawyers reveals how its board of trustees makes decisions.
item.content:encoded:
Last year, Trump dismissed Biden-appointed Kennedy Center board members and replaced them with loyalists. A new filing by the Center's lawyers reveals how its board of trustees makes decisions.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair
